kills: Conjecture 4 is vacuous: its hypothesis class is provably empty. A 6-critical H has min degree 5 (criticality), so |E(H)| >= 5n/2 (Kostochka-Yancey: >= 2.8n - 1.8). After a degree-5 split, |E(H-w)| >= 5n/2 - 5, but independence in the 2D rigidity matroid requires |E(H-w)| <= 2(n-1) - 3 = 2n - 5, and 5n/2 - 5 <= 2n - 5 forces n <= 0. So 'degree-5 split with H-w rigidity-independent with one flex' holds for NO 6-critical graph, the conjecture is vacuously true, and the advertised consequence (W3 decidable-in-practice via square transversal alpha systems) is unsupported for every actual candidate. Real candidates are over-determined frameworks (consistent with L52/P_510), where alpha on a square subsystem leaves surplus unit equations needing EXACT equality, which interval arithmetic can refute but never confirm; the 'without ever naming their field' selling point self-destructs precisely on the graphs that matter.

Conjecture 2's mechanism dies on a 7-to-8 vertex example. Explicit counterexample (computed and exactly verified this session, script C:\Users\Owen\AppData\Local\Temp\adv_clamp_k3.py): split the Moser spindle apex into s ~ {B1,B2}, t ~ {C1,C2} placed at the second circle-circle intersections. The resulting 8-vertex UDG has exactly 11 unit edges (exact sympy audit, no accidental edges or coincidences), all coordinates in Q(sqrt3, sqrt11) (s = ((sqrt11 + 11 sqrt3)/12, (-11 + sqrt33)/12)), |s-t|^2 = 11/3 so (s,t) is non-adjacent, chi = 3 with 36 proper 3-colorings and ZERO having c(s) = c(t), and G/(s=t) contains the full spindle. This is the exact Theorem-R clamp pattern at k=3, realized entirely by single-coincidence quadratic moves inside the lineage's own multiquadratic field. Therefore 'forcing requires imprimitive closure relations that transcend quadratic steps / exit 2-power Galois' is FALSE as a general law of unit-distance color forcing. Any true version of Conjecture 2 must be k=5-specific (degree >= 5 splits force max(|A|,|B|) >= 3, where cocircularity is genuinely codimension >= 1), a derivation the proposal asserts but never supplies.

Conjecture 3's 'verified' supporting fact is false: pure 7-torsion Cayley graphs are NOT bipartite. The partial sums of 1 + zeta_7 + ... + zeta_7^6 form a genuine 7-cycle of unit steps (verified: all 7 vertices distinct, min pairwise distance ~1.0, closes to 0), and 'coefficient parity' is ill-defined because the defining relation has odd weight 7. The correct statement: only generator edges exist (true, via Kronecker plus the abelian Galois group: any modulus-1 element of Z[zeta_7] is a root of unity) and chi is exactly 3 (functional with weights (1,1,1,1,1,2,2) mod 3). The design conclusion ('pure odd torsion is a trap') survives but the claimed verification did not happen correctly: a process flag on every other 'I verified' in the proposal.

Conjecture 3's engine Galois-collapses on first contact with its own recipe. I ran 8 hex-lattice double-coincidence towers exactly as specified (two rotations, two simultaneous unit coincidences, eliminate theta1 by sympy resultant; scripts adv_tower_probe.py, adv_tower_sweep.py): 2 were fully degenerate (the two coincidences dependent via lattice symmetry, solution set a curve, closure polynomial identically zero on the circle), and all 6 non-degenerate cases factored COMPLETELY into linear and 2-power-quadratic pieces, with all real closure angles at 6-torsion values cos(theta2) in {-1/2, 1/2, 1}. Zero S4 quartics, zero cubics. The 'generic quartics have Galois S4' argument fails because these eliminations are norm-form-structured over the abelian field Q(omega): the hex seed the conjecture mandates is arithmetically self-defeating. This is empirical outcome (a) of the conjecture's own falsifiable_prediction.

Conjecture 1's near-term falsifiable prediction is vacuous: the 2-power audit of the 12 lineage graphs confirms what is true BY CONSTRUCTION (they were built in declared multiquadratic fields), so passing it provides zero evidence for the upper-bound content of chi(C^2) = 5; the standing refutation route requires exactly the objects nobody has. As stated the conjecture has no in-repo discriminating test in either direction.

Category slip underlying Conjectures 1-3: color forcing is a property of the ABSTRACT graph; coordinates and their Galois type only filter which abstract graphs realize. Conjecture 3's prediction that an S4-field ball will show non-free pairs has no mechanism connecting field to forcing, and it bundles two independent miracles: (i) a new-principle chi-5 UDG at <= 4000 vertices (this is W3-adjacent open difficulty relocated, not reduced) and (ii) that the field, rather than combinatorial structure, controls pair-freeness. The Gerbracht-Harborth precedent (rigid matchstick UDG with degree-22, hence non-constructible, coordinates and no chromatic payoff whatsoever) shows field exit buys nothing chromatic by itself.

Axiom hygiene violation (Arch 4 discipline): the load-bearing staircase identity chi(R^2) = sup_K chi(K^2) uses de Bruijn-Erdos compactness (full AC) twice, plus Tarski transfer; no axiom set is declared. Under the brief's own dark horse (the definability split, chi_B), compactness fails and the entire staircase frame dissolves; the proposal never registers this.

Experiment design cannot decide what it claims: Phase 2's 6-9 vertex toys cannot host k=5 clamps (abstract witness is 48 vertices; chi=5 UDGs start around 500), so 'Conjecture 2 dies on the spot' is unreachable at the stated scale; rhombus chains have rational (degenerate) flex parametrizations, biasing closure Galois statistics toward collapse; and sympy galois_group caps at degree 6 while real double-coincidence closures rationalize to degree 8-12 (my instances hit 12), so the stated tool fails on its own outputs. Repairable via the mod-p odd-factor-degree certificate for non-2-power Galois, which I implemented and validated in the sweep.
prior_art_collisions: Subfield chromatic staircase is an established program, not a new lens: Woodall 1973 (Q^2 bipartite), Benda-Perles (Q^3, Q^4), Chilakamarri (Q^n and the L^infty plane), Fischer 1990 (additive K-colorable quadratic extensions, cited by the proposal), P.D. Johnson's rational-coloring program. The proposal extrapolates this line; only the constructible-plateau naming appears new.

Polymath16 ring-minimization threads and Parts 2020 (graph minimization for 5-chromatic UDGs in explicit quadratic rings) already treat 'which ring/field hosts chi=5' as a first-class question; the multiquadratic character of the entire lineage is known there, weakening the novelty of the audit half of Conjecture 1.

Tarski transfer to real-algebraic coordinates for finite UDGs is folklore used by Polymath16; the staircase identity additionally needs de Bruijn-Erdos, whose AC-dependence is exactly the Shelah-Soifer caveat this repo logs under Architecture 4.

Gerbracht 2006-2007: exact Harborth matchstick-graph coordinates with degree-22 minimal polynomials: rigid unit-distance graphs outside the constructible field already exist in the literature, with zero chromatic payoff: relevant uncited prior art for both the 'deliberately exit C' novelty claim and the field-equals-chromatic-power narrative.

Hauenstein-Sottile alphaCertified (2012) and certified numerical algebraic geometry in rigidity theory (e.g. Laman-graph realization counting, Capco-Gallet-Grasegger-Koutschan-Lubbes-Schicho): Conjecture 4's toolchain is standard there, including the known hard case (over-determined systems), which is precisely where HN candidates live per L52.

Lam-Leung vanishing sums of roots of unity plus Kronecker's theorem: govern the 7-torsion claims; the only-generator-edges fact is a standard abelian-field argument, and Lam-Leung is what produces the odd 7-cycle that falsifies the bipartiteness claim.
Kostochka-Yancey k-critical edge bounds: published arithmetic that makes Conjecture 4's hypothesis class emptier still (|E| >= 2.8n - 1.8 for 6-critical graphs).
result.surviving_kernel: Three things survive. (1) The constructible plateau QUESTION: chi(C^2) for C the real constructible numbers is, as far as I can verify, unnamed in the literature, well-posed, has lower bound 5 for free from de Grey, and gives the program a clean intermediate object between the exhausted multiquadratic lineage and R^2; the verified fact that every single-coincidence spindling step is quadratic (this claim checks out) makes 'the whole lineage lies in C' a true and useful boundary statement that complements L57's demand for a new principle. (2) The honest k=5-specific residue of Conjecture 2: degree >= 5 splits force an interface side of size >= 3, where unit-cocircularity is a genuine codimension >= 1 closure; whether THAT closure can land at constructible points compatible with forcing is open and is the right sharpened question (my k=3 counterexample shows nothing weaker survives). (3) Exact-rational Smale alpha certification as a Groebner replacement, correctly scoped: certify a minimally-rigid spanning subframework (square system; the Moser 11x11 calibration is sound), then prove surplus unit equations exactly via PSLQ/LLL minpoly recovery plus resultants, or via symmetry; plus my mod-p odd-factor-degree certificate for non-2-power Galois past sympy's degree-6 limit. As tooling this is genuinely new to the program and feasible on this host.
result.cheapest_decisive_test: A systematic tower sweep, about an hour of compute, deciding whether the non-2-power seeding route has any fuel: enumerate double-coincidence closure systems beyond the bilinear cross-coincidence pattern (include composed rotations R2*R1, mixed radii, and non-hex seeds such as the Moser spindle vertex set and Q(sqrt3,sqrt11) lineage fragments as base configurations), eliminate one angle by resultant, rationalize, factor over Q, and test every factor of degree >= 3 for non-2-power Galois via the factor-degrees-mod-p certificate (an odd factor degree > 1 mod any good prime is a rigorous non-2-power witness), keeping only factors with a real root in [-1,1]. My 8-instance pilot (scripts in C:\Users\Owen\AppData\Local\Temp\: adv_tower_probe.py, adv_tower_sweep.py) gave 8/8 collapse or degeneracy. If a ~100-instance sweep still yields zero non-2-power real closure angles, Conjectures 1-3 lose their only actionable content and the lens reduces to bookkeeping; a single surviving instance hands BUILDER a concrete seed angle and revives Conjecture 3 in repaired form.
result.steelman: Best defensible version: (a) Define the chromatic staircase rigorously in ZFC (de Bruijn-Erdos plus Tarski, axioms declared) and pose chi(C^2) as a named open question with known bounds 5 <= chi(C^2) <= chi(R^2) <= 7; claim novelty only for the question, crediting Woodall-Fischer-Benda-Perles-Polymath16 for the program. (b) Replace the false general mechanism with the sharp k=5 statement: in any clamp from a degree >= 5 split, one interface side has >= 3 vertices, so Theorem R's closure includes a genuine codimension >= 1 cocircularity condition along the flex of an over-determined H-w; conjecture ONLY that this specific closure locus contains no constructible point at which the abstract forcing graph realizes, and record explicitly that the k=3 analog fails (8-vertex Moser-split clamp in Q(sqrt3,sqrt11), this report) so any proof must use the width >= 3 geometry, not field generalities. (c) Demote the hex tower to one seed among many: since hex double-coincidence eliminations are norm forms over the abelian Q(omega) and empirically collapse to torsion and quadratic angles, seed instead from non-abelian-friendly base configurations (lineage fragments in Q(sqrt3,sqrt11), spindle chains, mixed-radius composed rotations), with the mod-p certificate as the non-2-power detector; treat 'does ANY realizable closure exit 2-power Galois' as the first gate before any chromatic claim. (d) Rebuild the certification pipeline for the over-determined reality: alpha-certify a square minimally-rigid spanning subsystem, recover candidate minimal polynomials by LLL from the certified approximation, verify surplus unit equations exactly by resultants in the recovered field, and use symmetry-forced mechanisms when the flex must survive dependence. This version makes no false claims, has two runnable gates that can each kill it cheaply, and retains the one real strategic insight: the exhausted lineage occupies a proper, field-theoretically definable corner of configuration space, and the search should be aimed, measurably, outside it.
